preamble: Vowel lowering of the high back rounded vowel [u] to the mid back rounded vowel [o] is a common historical sound change, often occurring in unstressed positions or triggered by the presence of low vowels in subsequent syllables.

examples.note: Short [u] in Latin regularly lowered to [o] in Spanish and other Romance languages.
Lowering of the high back vowel in the transition to Spanish.
The 'a-Umlaut' process lowered /u/ to /o/ when followed by a low vowel /a/ in the next syllable.
